prose: MPTP is called positive on the strength of curated CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence [E1], the diagnostic anchor that fixes this call and its curated-anchored-only tier. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis proceeds from a molecular initiating event grounded in MitoCarta3.0 Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the adverse outcome resolved in the Kamath 2022 SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2], and an attached AOP pathway lends structural coherence to this chain. Brain-penetrance predictions support plausible CNS target exposure [E5], a necessary precondition rather than proof of harm. Historically, human case series described causal parkinsonism following exposure [E4], consistent with—but not substituting for—the curated mechanism. ToxCast coverage is thin, MPTP being handled as a research reagent [E3]; this assay sparseness is anti-diagnostic and must not be read as reassuring or decisive. Overall the mechanistic story is internally consistent, though it rests principally on the curated anchor rather than broad orthogonal corroboration.

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
